purpose: Small focused follow-up after the Autopilot full-flow E2E work. The core propose/execute/artifact/completion flow is now well covered, but two hardening findings remain: ToolCallingLLMStrategy content fallback must not bypass shell-execution policy, and the runtime-selection migration should be portable and reversible. Optional: add a real VerificationService/Pytest integration test beyond synthetic verified artifacts.

positive_state: TaskScoped route-level persist/execute reload test exists.
Full-flow local E2E exists: propose -> execute_local_step -> files -> synthesized artifacts -> completion.
sgpt is blocked in default propose tests.
verification_required=True is tested at TaskCompletionPolicyService level with synthetic verified/unverified artifacts.
LLM response normalization was improved for inline JSON, inline bash and German advisory prose.
remaining_findings: ToolCallingLLMStrategy content fallback currently calls LLMResponseNormalizer.normalize(..., allow_shell_execution=True). This can bypass ProposePolicy.allow_shell_execution and turn model text into executable shell commands even when policy should deny shell execution.
Migration c6d7e8f9a0b1 uses PostgreSQL-specific server_default=sa.text('''[]'''::json') and downgrade() is pass. This can break SQLite/test environments and makes rollback unsafe.
Existing verification_required=True test uses synthetic artifact verification_status values. A real VerificationService/Pytest integration test would provide stronger evidence.

tasks.title: Make ToolCallingLLMStrategy content fallback respect ProposePolicy.allow_shell_execution
tasks.problem: When provider returns no native tool_calls but provides text content, ToolCallingLLMStrategy currently sends content to LLMResponseNormalizer with allow_shell_execution=True. This can convert inline or fenced shell commands into ExecutableProposal even when policy disallows shell execution.
implementation_steps: In ToolCallingLLMStrategy, derive allow_shell_execution from context.policy.allow_shell_execution when present.
Default allow_shell_execution to false if context.policy is missing or does not expose the field.
Call normalizer.normalize(content, context, allow_shell_execution=allow_shell_execution) instead of hardcoded True.
Ensure fallback metadata records source=tool_calling_llm_content_fallback and allow_shell_execution value for audit.
If content contains shell and policy denies shell, fallback must return advisory/declined, not executable.
Keep tool_call JSON fallback possible when content contains valid tool_calls; shell denial must not block safe write_file/tool_call JSON if tool policy allows it.
acceptance_criteria: Content fallback with inline shell and allow_shell_execution=false returns non-executable advisory/declined result.
Content fallback with inline shell and allow_shell_execution=true can return executable command, if command policy also accepts it.
Content fallback with valid JSON tool_calls can still return executable tool_calls without enabling shell execution.
Metadata includes source_format and policy-derived allow_shell_execution value.
No hardcoded allow_shell_execution=True remains in ToolCallingLLMStrategy.
security_notes: Model text is untrusted input. It must never become a shell command unless policy explicitly allows shell execution.
Tool calls are still subject to allowed tool and path policies; this task only fixes the shell fallback flag.

tasks.title: Add regression tests for content fallback policy behavior
tasks.problem: The policy-bypass risk needs tests so future prompt/normalizer changes cannot reintroduce it.
implementation_steps: Mock ModelInvocationService.invoke_with_tools to return {tool_calls: [], content: '`rm -rf /tmp/x`', finish_reason: 'stop'}.
Build ProposeContext with policy.allow_shell_execution=false and assert result is not executable.
Repeat with policy.allow_shell_execution=true and a harmless command like `echo ok`; assert executable only in this case.
Add a valid inline JSON tool_calls case and assert it works without shell permission.
Add a fenced shell block case to ensure both inline and fenced shell follow the same policy.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail against the current hardcoded True implementation.
Tests pass after APH-T001.
Tests cover inline bash, fenced bash, inline JSON command and inline JSON tool_calls.

tasks.title: Make runtime-selection Alembic migration portable across SQLite/Postgres
tasks.problem: Migration c6d7e8f9a0b1 adds agents.runtime_targets with server_default=sa.text('''[]'''::json'), which is PostgreSQL-specific and likely invalid on SQLite. Tests/dev often use SQLite.

implementation_options.description: Use SQLAlchemy JSON column without DB-specific server_default and rely on application-level default [] for agents.runtime_targets.
Branch by dialect: PostgreSQL uses '''[]'''::json or safer sa.text('''[]'''), SQLite uses sa.text('''[]''') or no server_default depending on compatibility.

tasks.recommended_option: A unless database-level non-null default is truly required.
implementation_steps: Remove PostgreSQL-only ::json cast from server_default or gate it by dialect.
Verify nullable/default behavior for existing rows.
Ensure application/model layer initializes runtime_targets to [] when missing/null.
Add migration smoke test for SQLite if test harness supports Alembic migration execution.
Add a Postgres-oriented assertion/documentation if CI supports Postgres.
acceptance_criteria: Migration upgrade runs on SQLite test database.
Migration upgrade remains valid for Postgres.
agents.runtime_targets is never observed as None in application code after loading/normalization.
No raw PostgreSQL-only cast is executed on SQLite.

tasks.title: Implement real downgrade or explicit irreversible migration guard
tasks.problem: Migration c6d7e8f9a0b1 has downgrade() pass. For schema changes, this hides rollback behavior and can confuse dev/prod recovery.
implementation_steps: Implement _drop_column_if_exists helper mirroring _add_column_if_missing.
In downgrade(), drop worker_results selection columns, worker_jobs selection columns and agents.runtime_targets in reverse order.
If rollback must be intentionally unsupported, replace pass with a clear RuntimeError explaining why it is irreversible. Prefer real downgrade for this migration.
acceptance_criteria: downgrade() is no longer silent pass.
Either downgrade removes the added columns safely if tables/columns exist, or it fails loudly with explicit irreversible reason.
No data-destructive behavior occurs accidentally without clear migration intent.

tasks.title: Add optional real VerificationService/Pytest E2E for generated Fibonacci project
tasks.problem: Current verification_required=True coverage uses synthetic artifact verification_status fields. A real VerificationService/Pytest integration test would prove generated project tests can be executed and reflected into completion evidence.
implementation_steps: Use existing mocked LLM full-flow to create app.py and tests/test_app.py.
Run VerificationService or a controlled pytest command in the isolated workspace.
Map successful verification output to artifact verification_status='verified' or equivalent collection_result shape.
Evaluate TaskCompletionPolicyService with verification_required=true.
Add negative case where generated test fails and completion is not completed.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path runs real verification and completes only when verification passes.
Failing generated tests produce needs_review/retryable_failed/failed according to policy.
The test remains deterministic and does not contact a real LLM/network.
notes: This is optional because policy-level verification gating is already tested synthetically, but real verification integration gives stronger confidence.

tasks.title: Update documentation and TODO status after hardening
tasks.problem: After APH-T001..T005, the TODO/DOC files should accurately say what is proven: proposal, execute, artifact completion, policy shell gating and migration portability.
implementation_steps: Mark APH tasks done only after code and tests are present.
Document that tool_calling native tool_calls are preferred; content fallback is policy-gated.
Document that shell execution from model content is denied by default.
Document migration portability decision.
acceptance_criteria: Docs/TODOs do not overclaim real verification if APH-T005 remains optional/todo.
Policy behavior is clear enough for future implementers.

global_done_definition: ToolCallingLLMStrategy content fallback never hardcodes shell execution permission.
Shell commands extracted from model content are advisory/non-executable unless ProposePolicy.allow_shell_execution is true and command policy accepts them.
Safe JSON tool_calls fallback still works without enabling shell execution.
Runtime-selection migration upgrades on SQLite/dev and Postgres/prod or clearly gates dialect differences.
Migration downgrade is no longer silent pass unless explicitly irreversible with clear error.
Optional: real VerificationService/Pytest E2E proves verification_required=true with generated project tests.

summary_bottom.assessment: The Autopilot flow is now broadly strong. This hardening TODO exists because one recent improvement introduced a shell policy bypass risk in ToolCallingLLMStrategy content fallback, and the runtime-target migration needs portability cleanup.
summary_bottom.most_important_next_step: Fix APH-T001/APH-T002 first: derive allow_shell_execution from context.policy, never hardcode True.
